source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 12.1-12.2 cron basics (pp. 210-211)

questions.q: What is the cron utility used for?
questions.why: cron schedules the periodic execution of tasks (backups, monitoring, repetitive admin jobs); you interact with it through crontab.

options: Administrators for system tasks, and normal users for tasks/scripts they have access to (subject to permission)
questions.why: cron is used by admins for system tasks and by normal users for their own, controlled by the cron.allow/cron.deny permission files.

questions.q: Which daemon runs the cron service in memory?
questions.why: The crond daemon runs the cron service; if it is not running, scheduled tasks will not launch.

questions.q: Check the status of the crond service.
questions.why: systemctl status crond shows whether the cron daemon is running.

questions.q: Enable the crond daemon so it starts automatically at boot.
questions.why: Enabling crond ensures the scheduler is running after a reboot, so scheduled tasks actually fire.
